description: Covert DNS exfiltration tunnel that leverages standard UDP/53 DNS query resolution to exfiltrate collected binary blobs out of restricted networks by encoding payload chunks into authoritative FQDN requests.

capabilities: Custom DNS packet builder for stealthy 'A' record queries without external DNS libraries
Dynamic payload fragmentation respecting the 63-character RFC limit per label
Base32 (RFC-compliant label safe) and Hexadecimal encoding support
Automatic session ID generation for parallel exfiltration multiplexing
INIT, Chunked-Stream, and EOF signaling control channels
Jitter implementation on query delays to evade volume-based anomaly detection
Fallback to OS-native local stub resolvers if direct UDP outbound is blocked
evasion_techniques: Uses standard OS DNS resolver by default to blend with regular application traffic
Base32 encoding avoids suspicious characters in the Doman Name field
Timing jitter prevents simple frequency analysis detections
Queries 'A' records instead of noisy 'TXT' or 'NULL' records usually used by tunnels

detection_opportunities: High volume of DNS requests to a single authoritative domain
Abnormally long, randomly-generated subdomains (high entropy in DNS query names)
Significant spike in TXT or A record queries from a single host that never receive answers
DNS requests originating from unusual or non-browser processes
